articles.title: Taylor Frankie Paul's 'Bachelorette' Men Under 'Bachelor' Consideration - TMZ
articles.description: Casting for the next 'Bachelor' is in overdrive ... and among the men under consideration to be the next face of the franchise are contestants from Taylor Frankie Paul's shelved 'Bachelorette' season.

articles.title: Horoscope for Wednesday, 05/27/26 by Christopher Renstrom - SFGATE
articles.description: TAURUS (April 19 - May 19): You've been there for a partner. Now it's time for your partner to return the favor. All you have to do is ask.

articles.title: BTS is releasing limited edition hotteok-flavored Oreos - KSL.com
articles.description: K-pop group BTS returned in March with their first concert in nearly four years. Now it's teaming up with Oreo, one of America's most famous cookie brands, for a limited-edition flavor.

articles.title: John McClain, Michael Jackson Estate Co-Executor, Dead at 71 - TMZ
articles.description: John McClain, the co-executor of Michael Jackson's estate and a veteran music executive and producer has died ... TMZ has learned.

articles.title: Kevin Hart Addresses Backlash to Tony Hinchcliffe’s Roast Jokes: “Stop Talking as If I Said It” - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.description: "Yeah, the George Floyd joke, it wasn't a tasteful joke to our culture, to our audience, but our audience that's watching the roast, if you're watching the roast, you get why they're doing it," the comedian said.

articles.title: ‘Chess’ To Close Early On Broadway Next Month With Departure Of Lea Michele - Deadline
articles.description: Chess, the Broadway musical revival, will close June 21, three months earlier than expected and the final performance of star Lea Michele.

articles.description: 'Obsession' made an additional $23.9 million in its second weekend in release, an increase of 39 percent, putting it in rare company.

articles.title: Jimin Edits BTS’ AMAs Artist of the Year Winner Card to Show Who He Thinks Should’ve Won Instead - Billboard

articles.title: If you attend a David Sedaris reading, you're helping him edit - NPR
articles.description: Sedaris says the best part of reading his work to an audience is earning the laughs — or the groans. "A collective groan is fine with me," he says. His new book is The Land and Its People.
